sources.title: MASt3R-SLAM: Real-Time Dense SLAM with 3D Reconstruction Priors
sources.authors: Riku Murai; Eric Dexheimer; Andrew J. Davison
sources.summary: Primary anchor for turning a two-view 3D prior into a real-time dense SLAM pipeline under a generic central camera assumption.
sources.notes: Best single conceptual anchor for the new report fragment.

sources.title: ViSTA-SLAM: Visual SLAM with Symmetric Two-view Association
sources.authors: Ganlin Zhang; Shenhan Qian; Xi Wang; Daniel Cremers
sources.summary: Primary uncalibrated monocular reference with a symmetric two-view frontend and Sim(3) pose graph backend.
sources.notes: Strongest alignment with the explicit unknown-intrinsics challenge.

sources.title: DROID-SLAM: Deep Visual SLAM for Monocular, Stereo, and RGB-D Cameras
sources.summary: Optimization anchor for dense bundle adjustment in learned SLAM.
sources.notes: Use as theory contrast for dense optimization.

sources.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering
sources.authors: Bernhard Kerbl; Georgios Kopanas; Thomas Leimkuehler; George Drettakis
sources.summary: Endpoint representation for the report: explicit anisotropic Gaussians, projected covariance, and visibility-aware alpha compositing.
sources.notes: Use as downstream stage, not as the first-stage benchmark target.

sources.title: Bundle Adjustment --- A Modern Synthesis
sources.authors: Bill Triggs; Philip F. McLauchlan; Richard I. Hartley; Andrew W. Fitzgibbon
sources.summary: Classical optimization reference for joint structure-and-motion estimation.
sources.notes: Use for the canonical bundle adjustment equation.

sources.title: Multiple View Geometry in Computer Vision
sources.summary: Classical reference for projective geometry, camera models, and multiview reasoning.
sources.notes: Use for the geometric foundations paragraph.

sources.title: Structure-from-Motion Revisited
sources.summary: Classical SfM and camera-calibration grounding; also the initialization route assumed by the original 3DGS paper.
sources.notes: Important for explaining why 3DGS is downstream of pose recovery.

sources.title: Wikipedia: Bundle adjustment
sources.notes: Do not use as primary evidence in the scientific narrative.

sources.title: Wikipedia: Structure from motion
sources.notes: Useful only for intuitive reading links.

sources.title: Wikipedia: Projective geometry
sources.summary: Grounding page for projective geometry intuition.
sources.notes: Useful only for intuitive reading links.

sources.title: Wikipedia: Simultaneous localization and mapping
sources.notes: Useful only for intuitive reading links.

concepts.name: Bundle adjustment
concepts.definition: Joint nonlinear least-squares optimization over camera parameters and scene structure.
concepts.why_it_matters: Provides the canonical objective that modern dense SLAM systems generalize or approximate.

concepts.name: Projective geometry
concepts.definition: Geometric framework for image formation, projection, and multiview constraints.
concepts.why_it_matters: Grounds camera projection models and the transition from 3D structure to image measurements.

concepts.name: Structure from motion
concepts.definition: Recovery of camera poses and scene structure from multiple views.
concepts.why_it_matters: Provides the initialization logic assumed by COLMAP and the original 3DGS paper.

concepts.name: Sim(3) pose graph optimization
concepts.definition: Pose-graph optimization over similarity transforms to absorb rotation, translation, and scale drift.
concepts.why_it_matters: Crucial for monocular SLAM because scale is only weakly constrained locally.

concepts.name: Generic central camera
concepts.definition: A camera model in which all rays pass through a single camera center without requiring a fixed low-parameter pinhole form.
concepts.why_it_matters: Matches the weak calibration assumptions of MASt3R-SLAM and the smartphone-video challenge.
concepts.notes: Wikipedia link is adjacent grounding, not a perfect one-to-one concept page.

concepts.name: Pointmap
concepts.definition: Dense per-pixel or per-location 3D point predictions associated with an image or image pair.
concepts.why_it_matters: Provides the bridge from two-view learned priors to dense SLAM backends.

concepts.name: Ray-space residual
concepts.definition: Residual defined over viewing directions instead of raw pixel coordinates or absolute depth.
concepts.why_it_matters: More robust when calibration and metric scale are uncertain.

concepts.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting
concepts.definition: Scene representation using explicit anisotropic 3D Gaussians rendered through projected covariances and alpha compositing.
concepts.why_it_matters: Natural downstream representation once poses and coarse geometry are stable.

concepts.name: Loop closure
concepts.definition: Detection and incorporation of revisited places to suppress drift and improve global consistency.
concepts.why_it_matters: Necessary for long monocular trajectories and consistent dense maps.

equations.name: Classical bundle adjustment objective
equations.latex: E_{BA} = \sum_{(i,j) \in \mathcal O} \rho\left(e_{ij}^\top e_{ij}\right), \quad e_{ij} = \pi(K_i, T_i X_j) - u_{ij}
equations.symbol_legend: T_i: camera pose; X_j: 3D point; u_ij: image observation; K_i: intrinsics; pi: projection; rho: robust loss.
equations.conceptual_explanation: States that the best reconstruction is the one whose projected 3D points explain the image measurements after robustly down-weighting outliers.

equations.name: Sim(3) pose graph objective
equations.latex: E_{PGO} = \sum_{(i,j) \in \mathcal E} r_{ij}^\top \Omega_{ij} r_{ij}, \quad r_{ij} = \log\left(\hat{S}_{ij}^{-1} S_i^{-1} S_j\right)
equations.symbol_legend: S_i: node pose in Sim(3); \hat{S}_{ij}: measured relative constraint; \Omega_{ij}: information weight.
equations.conceptual_explanation: Explains how monocular SLAM can absorb scale drift and enforce global consistency through loop-closure constraints over similarity transforms.

equations.name: Ray-space residual for weak calibration
equations.symbol_legend: X_{i,m}: predicted 3D point at location m; T_ij: relative transform; ray(·): viewing-direction normalization.
equations.conceptual_explanation: Uses direction agreement rather than exact pixel reprojection or metric depth, which is safer when calibration is uncertain.

equations.name: 3D Gaussian parameterization
equations.latex: G(x) = \exp\left(-\frac{1}{2} x^\top \Sigma^{-1} x\right), \quad \Sigma = R S S^\top R^\top
equations.symbol_legend: x: local coordinate; \Sigma: covariance; R: rotation; S: scaling matrix.
equations.conceptual_explanation: Defines each splat as an anisotropic ellipsoid with a valid positive semidefinite covariance.

equations.name: Projected covariance of a 3D Gaussian
equations.symbol_legend: W: viewing transform; J: Jacobian of the local projective mapping; \Sigma': image-space covariance.
equations.conceptual_explanation: Shows how a world-space Gaussian footprint becomes a 2D screen-space ellipse for splatting.

equations.name: Front-to-back alpha compositing for 3DGS
equations.symbol_legend: C(p): pixel color; c_i: Gaussian color or SH appearance; \alpha_i: opacity contribution; \mathcal N(p): depth-ordered Gaussians affecting p.
equations.conceptual_explanation: Preserves the logic of volumetric rendering while remaining compatible with fast rasterization.

recommendations.title: Separate settings from run configs
recommendations.recommendation: Keep machine-local paths and checkpoints in settings models, while run requests and stage configs stay in experiment configs.
recommendations.rationale: Prevents environment-specific state from contaminating benchmark artifacts.

recommendations.title: Prefer SLAM first, 3DGS second
recommendations.recommendation: Recover poses and coarse geometry before fitting a 3DGS stage.
recommendations.rationale: Matches the assumptions of the original 3DGS paper and preserves diagnostic power in evaluation.

recommendations.title: Keep calibrated and uncalibrated branches explicit
recommendations.recommendation: Model calibrated and uncalibrated pipelines as distinct execution branches with distinct residuals and expectations.
recommendations.rationale: Pixel-space and ray-space objectives solve related but different problems.

recommendations.title: Benchmark ViSTA-SLAM and MASt3R-SLAM jointly
recommendations.recommendation: Use MASt3R-SLAM as the main conceptual anchor and ViSTA-SLAM as the strongest uncalibrated comparison.
recommendations.rationale: The pair spans dense learned SLAM and explicit uncalibrated Sim(3) reasoning.

recommendations.title: Store frame conventions and units
recommendations.recommendation: Every normalized artifact should declare coordinate frame, scale convention, units, and timestamp basis.
recommendations.rationale: Downstream rendering and evaluation break quickly when geometry metadata is implicit.

recommendations.title: Evaluate trajectory, geometry, and rendering separately
recommendations.recommendation: Do not let good novel-view synthesis hide poor pose recovery or poor geometric fidelity.
recommendations.rationale: These surfaces answer different scientific questions.

report_fragments.intent: Provide a theory-rich, design-oriented bridge from monocular dense SLAM to downstream 3DGS for the project report.
report_fragments.notes: Standalone fragment, not yet included in docs/report/main.typ.

source_concepts.evidence: Canonical reference for joint structure-and-motion optimization.
Classical treatment of projection and multiview geometry.
Classical SfM initialization route also assumed by 3DGS.
Uses a Sim(3) pose graph with loop closure in the uncalibrated monocular setting.
Assumes only a generic central camera rather than a fixed parametric model.
Uses ray-space reasoning for uncalibrated or weakly calibrated tracking and optimization.
Builds the SLAM system around a two-view 3D prior that predicts pointmaps.
Defines anisotropic 3D Gaussians, projected covariances, and alpha compositing for novel-view synthesis.
Loop closure is a central part of the global-consistency story.

source_equations.evidence: Defines the canonical optimization target for multiview reconstruction.
Explains why Sim(3) is the correct group for monocular drift and scale ambiguity.
Ray-space residual is central to the weak-calibration story.
Defines each 3D Gaussian and its covariance parameterization.
Shows how world-space covariance becomes a screen-space footprint.
Shows that 3DGS rendering preserves the volumetric alpha-compositing logic.

source_recommendations.evidence: The original method assumes poses and sparse points are already available.
The paper explicitly distinguishes uncalibrated ray-space reasoning from calibrated pixel-space refinement.
Provides the strongest complementary uncalibrated monocular baseline.
Dense BA and rendering quality solve different problems and should not be conflated.
